pages.raw_text:
The Northwest Civic Improve
ment club met last night at the
Presbyterian church, corner Of
Gardner and Cannon, to elect dele
gates to the Federated Mems clubs
executive committee and to dis.
cuss issues of the coming city
campaign. The club expects to
take part with other mems clubs
of the city with view to further
Improvement in municipal condi-
lions.


James T. Burcham and Russell
F. Collins were chosen delegates
to the executive committee and
instructed to urge the following
matters in the executive commit.
tee of the Federated Mems clubs.


Amendment of the city charter
to provide for the recall. 1t was
the sense of the club that a recall
election should be had by a petit
ton of not to exceed Is per cent
of the vote at the last previous
election.


The reduction of the vote re-
quired for instituting the initiative
and referendum from 35 per cent
as at present to Is per cent.


Vigorous action by the Federated
Mems clubs to procure the passage
by the legislature of the municipal
primary law recently proposed by
the charter revision committee
This law was read in full to the
club and the members were unani
nous in their strong approval OF
this provision. This Is the law tina,
has recently been proposed b,
Senator Graves Of Spokane, and
the club believes that every effort
should DO made to procure favor
